_comment: gen316 (forge-master-plan W0.2): machine-readable benchmark manifest. Semantics: 'gate' = ratchet, must be green before any promotion; 'discovery' = instrument that surfaces gaps, red rows are pulls, not build failures; 'degrade' = harness runs but the capability is NOT engaged (a PASS measures the harness, never the capability); 'external' = needs resources outside the repo (LLM API, dataset download, docker), excluded from offline gating.
_capabilities_comment: gen317 (W0.3): faculty maturity claims (forge plan §4: ABSENT/SEED/TRANSFER/FIELD/HARDENED) with the benchmarks that evidence them. make capability-report VERIFIES each claim against real results: a red gate in the evidence downgrades the claim to REGRESSED; evidence that is only discovery/external is reported as claimed-unverified. The ledger is generated, never hand-written.
_audit_comment: gen320 (§15 row 3): the manifest is now AUDITED, because it was lying (§3.4). Three fields make each claim mechanically checkable, and `make manifest-audit` (in `make test`) goes red on a false one: 'script' = the file the target actually runs (it must exist — 'basic-chat' named tests/basic_chat_bench.sh, which does not); 'exit_contract' = red-on-failure (the exit code is load-bearing) or always-zero (an instrument that reports, never fails); 'requires' = the external resources the target genuinely needs. A 'gate' whose script cannot exit non-zero is not a gate; an 'external' that touches no external resource is not external. Fixed in this generation: glue was gated but always exited 0; mimic/mmlu/bbh were called external while running entirely on local fixtures, offline.
